gpu: AMD Radeon RX 7600 (radeonsi, navi33, LLVM 20.1.6, DRM 3.61, 6.14.9-hardened1-1-hardened)
notes:
I installed the game fine. Launched it ok. Went through the installer to install the launcher. The drop-down menus bugged out the UI when I clicked on them, but I was able to select options with the arrow keys once a menu was selected. I was able to install the launcher and create a Rockstar Games account.

Then it gets stuck in a loading loop of 3 different windows and never progresses to the actual launcher. I have tried the default Proton version (which I believe is Experimental), Proton 9.0-4, and Proton 8.0-5. I've also tried Cryo's suggestion: https://www.protondb.com/app/1174180#rf9yqNadbQ

None of the suggested fixes got it working for me. I'm quite sad to not be able to play this game especially since I paid for it based on its gold ProtonDB rating. On Artix Linux and Wayland.
title: Red Dead Redemption 2

notes: Works great out of the box only a bit of lag on menus and some object pop in

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT (radeonsi, navi22, LLVM 19.1.7, DRM 3.61, 6.12.33+deb13-amd64)
notes:
If game launches, a black screen is shown and a gold revolver icon is shown in the bottom right until game is closed. Unable to progress to main menu.

 No other issues, besides occasional softlocks where the game is not launched, with progressing through the R* launcher  

In `steamapps/compatdata/1174180/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Documents/Rockstar Games/Social Club/ipc.log`: 
```log
[0709/144522:WARNING:ipc_channel_win.cc(169)] failed to create pipe: 2
[0709/144522:WARNING:ipc_channel_win.cc(46)] Unable to create pipe named "rgsc_launcher_0" in client mode.
```

Switching Proton versions, clearing the prefix, and additional commandline arguments do not resolve this. Analysing errors via PROTON_LOG yielded nothing actionable. Suspecting an issue with the latest R* launcher update (25 Jun)
title: Red Dead Redemption 2
